description: Migraine in a young adult with classic features and normal vitals; defensible ESI 3 (workup needed to exclude secondary) or ESI 4 (typical migraine).

patient.name: Anna H., 26F
patient.chief_complaint: Severe right-sided throbbing migraine for 6 hours, classic pattern same as past 8 episodes over 3 years. Photophobia, nausea, no vomiting. No fever, no neck stiffness. Took sumatriptan with partial relief.
patient.history: Known migraine history (multiple prior similar episodes), otherwise healthy, on OCPs

rubric_notes: Classic migraine pattern matching prior episodes + stable vitals = low risk for secondary cause. Defensible ESI 3 (severe pain warrants prompt analgesia, IV access, possible IV fluids + anti-emetics = 2+ resources) or ESI 4 (typical migraine = symptom relief at fast_track, often single resource). OCP + smoker would elevate concern for atypical headache.
